payload.objective: Review the generated inbox runner together with its reviewed delegate script from the local inbox pipeline and provide a structured verdict using one of: pass, fail, needs_revision. Always output a review markdown artifact and include verdict in metadata.

params.title: BL-20260324-014 live preview smoke sample 2026-03-24 (best-effort reviewable attempt)
params.description:
Purpose:

  Controlled Trello live preview smoke for openclaw-team.

‌

  Expected behavior:

- read-only Trello ingest

- preview creation smoke only

- no business execution claim

- no Trello writeback expected in this step

‌

  Traceability:

- backlog: BL-20260324-014

- blocker context: BL-20260324-015

- created_by: Oscarling

- created_at: 2026-03-24 Asia/Shanghai

‌

  Note:

  This card is only for governed smoke verification and should remain open until the smoke is finished.

Execution contract: treat this as a best-effort, evidence-backed PDF extraction/conversion attempt. Do not claim OCR success without evidence. If full OCR/Excel conversion is not honestly achievable, return reviewable intermediate artifacts, explicit limitations, and next-step guidance. Keep behavior deterministic and limited to declared local artifacts.

review_scope.goal: Audit the wrapper and the reviewed delegate together so the review evidence can speak to the end-to-end readonly smoke path.

artifact_snapshots.content:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import json
import os
import subprocess
import sys
import tempfile
from dataclasses import dataclass
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any

DEFAULT_INPUT_DIR = "~/Desktop/pdf样本"
DEFAULT_OUTPUT_XLSX = "artifacts/outputs/trello_readonly/pdf_to_excel_from_trello.xlsx"
DEFAULT_OCR = "auto"
DEFAULT_DRY_RUN = False
DEFAULT_ORIGIN_ID = "trello:69c24cd3c1a2359ddd7a1bf8"
DEFAULT_TITLE = "BL-20260324-014 live preview smoke sample 2026-03-24 (best-effort reviewable attempt)"
DEFAULT_DESCRIPTION = (
    "Purpose:\n"
    "Controlled Trello live preview smoke for openclaw-team.\n"
    "Expected behavior:\n"
    "- read-only Trello ingest\n"
    "- preview creation smoke only\n"
    "- no business execution claim\n"
    "- no Trello writeback expected in this step\n"
    "Traceability:\n"
    "- backlog: BL-20260324-014\n"
    "- blocker context: BL-20260324-015\n"
    "- created_by: Oscarling\n"
    "- created_at: 2026-03-24 Asia/Shanghai\n"
    "Note:\n"
    "This card is only for governed smoke verification and should remain open until the smoke is finished."
)
DEFAULT_LABELS = ["best_effort", "evidence_backed", "readonly", "reviewable", "trello"]
DEFAULT_PREFERRED_BASE_SCRIPT = "artifacts/scripts/pdf_to_excel_ocr.py"
DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 600


@dataclass
class RunnerConfig:
    input_dir: str
    output_xlsx: str
    ocr: str
    dry_run: bool
    origin_id: str
    title: str
    description: str
    labels: list[str]
    preferred_base_script: str
    timeout_seconds: int


def _str_to_bool(value: str | bool) -> bool:
    if isinstance(value, bool):
        return value
    normalized = str(value).strip().lower()
    if normalized in {"1", "true", "yes", "y", "on"}:
        return True
    if normalized in {"0", "false", "no", "n", "off"}:
        return False
    raise argparse.ArgumentTypeError(f"invalid boolean value: {value}")


def parse_args() -> RunnerConfig:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        description="Reviewable wrapper for the reviewed PDF-to-Excel delegate."
    )
    parser.add_argument("--input-dir", default=DEFAULT_INPUT_DIR)
    parser.add_argument("--output-xlsx", default=DEFAULT_OUTPUT_XLSX)
    parser.add_argument("--ocr", default=DEFAULT_OCR)
    parser.add_argument("--dry-run", nargs="?", const="true", default=str(DEFAULT_DRY_RUN).lower())
    parser.add_argument("--origin-id", default=DEFAULT_ORIGIN_ID)
    parser.add_argument("--title", default=DEFAULT_TITLE)
    parser.add_argument("--description", default=DEFAULT_DESCRIPTION)
    parser.add_argument("--labels", nargs="*", default=DEFAULT_LABELS)
    parser.add_argument("--preferred-base-script", default=DEFAULT_PREFERRED_BASE_SCRIPT)
    parser.add_argument("--timeout-seconds", type=int, default=DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS)
    args = parser.parse_args()
    return RunnerConfig(
        input_dir=args.input_dir,
        output_xlsx=args.output_xlsx,
        ocr=args.ocr,
        dry_run=_str_to_bool(args.dry_run),
        origin_id=args.origin_id,
        title=args.title,
        description=args.description,
        labels=list(args.labels),
        preferred_base_script=args.preferred_base_script,
        timeout_seconds=max(1, int(args.timeout_seconds)),
    )


def script_dir() -> Path:
    return Path(__file__).resolve().parent


def repo_root() -> Path:
    return script_dir().parent.parent


def resolve_delegate(preferred_base_script: str) -> Path:
    candidate = Path(os.path.expanduser(preferred_base_script))
    if candidate.is_absolute():
        resolved = candidate
    else:
        roots = [repo_root(), script_dir()]
        resolved = None
        for root in roots:
            maybe = (root / candidate).resolve()
            if maybe.exists():
                resolved = maybe
                break
        if resolved is None:
            resolved = (repo_root() / candidate).resolve()
    return resolved


def discover_pdfs(input_dir: str) -> list[str]:
    root = Path(os.path.expanduser(input_dir)).resolve()
    if not root.exists() or not root.is_dir():
        return []
    return sorted(str(path) for path in root.rglob("*.pdf") if path.is_file())


def safe_json_loads(text: str) -> Any | None:
    text = text.strip()
    if not text:
        return None
    try:
        return json.loads(text)
    except json.JSONDecodeError:
        pass
    for line in reversed([line.strip() for line in text.splitlines() if line.strip()]):
        try:
            return json.loads(line)
        except json.JSONDecodeError:
            continue
    return None


def make_base_summary(config: RunnerConfig, pdfs: list[str]) -> dict[str, Any]:
    output_path = Path(os.path.expanduser(config.output_xlsx))
    return {
        "status": "partial",
        "request": {
            "origin_id": config.origin_id,
            "title": config.title,
            "description": config.description,
            "labels": config.labels,
            "ocr": config.ocr,
            "dry_run": config.dry_run,
        },
        "paths": {
            "input_dir": str(Path(os.path.expanduser(config.input_dir)).resolve()),
            "output_xlsx": str(output_path.resolve()),
        },
        "preflight": {
            "discovery_mode": "recursive_rglob_*.pdf",
            "discovered_pdf_count": len(pdfs),
            "discovered_pdfs": pdfs,
        },
        "execution": {
            "delegated": False,
            "delegate_script": None,
            "delegate_command": None,
            "timeout_seconds": config.timeout_seconds,
        },
        "attestation": {
            "excel_written": False,
            "output_exists": output_path.exists(),
            "output_size_bytes": output_path.stat().st_size if output_path.exists() and output_path.is_file() else 0,
        },
        "limitations": [],
        "next_steps": [],
    }


def build_delegate_command(config: RunnerConfig, delegate_script: Path, report_path: Path) -> list[str]:
    cmd = [
        sys.executable,
        str(delegate_script),
        "--input-dir",
        config.input_dir,
        "--output-xlsx",
        config.output_xlsx,
        "--ocr",
        config.ocr,
        "--report-json",
        str(report_path),
    ]
    if config.dry_run:
        cmd.append("--dry-run")
    return cmd


def evaluate_delegate_report(report: dict[str, Any], output_xlsx: str) -> tuple[str, dict[str, Any], list[str], list[str]]:
    limitations: list[str] = []
    next_steps: list[str] = []
    output_path = Path(os.path.expanduser(output_xlsx))

    status = report.get("status")
    total_files = report.get("total_files")
    dry_run = report.get("dry_run")
    status_counter = report.get("status_counter") or {}
    excel_written = bool(report.get("excel_written", False))
    output_exists = bool(report.get("output_exists", output_path.exists()))
    output_size_bytes = int(report.get("output_size_bytes", output_path.stat().st_size if output_path.exists() and output_path.is_file() else 0))

    attestation = {
        "excel_written": excel_written,
        "output_exists": output_exists,
        "output_size_bytes": output_size_bytes,
    }

    strong_success = (
        status == "success"
        and isinstance(total_files, int)
        and total_files >= 1
        and dry_run is False
        and int(status_counter.get("failed", 0)) == 0
        and int(status_counter.get("partial", 0)) == 0
        and excel_written is True
        and output_exists is True
        and output_size_bytes > 0
    )

    if strong_success:
        return "success", attestation, limitations, next_steps

    if status == "partial":
        limitations.append("Delegate reported partial outcome with structured evidence; wrapper preserves partial rather than overstating success.")
        next_steps.append("Review delegate report details and source PDFs to determine whether OCR/dependencies/input quality prevented full conversion.")
        if not excel_written:
            next_steps.append("If a workbook is required, rerun after confirming the reviewed delegate can write a real XLSX in this environment.")
        return "partial", attestation, limitations, next_steps

    if status == "success" and not strong_success:
        limitations.append("Delegate reported success but wrapper evidence gates for reviewable success were not fully satisfied.")
        next_steps.append("Inspect delegate report fields status_counter, excel_written, output_exists, and output_size_bytes before accepting the result as a true XLSX success.")
        return "partial", attestation, limitations, next_steps

    limitations.append("Delegate did not provide enough evidence for a successful reviewable XLSX conversion.")
    next_steps.append("Inspect delegate stdout/stderr/report and rerun after resolving missing dependencies, unsupported OCR path, or malformed input PDFs.")
    return "failed", attestation, limitations, next_steps


def main() -> int:
    config = parse_args()
    pdfs = discover_pdfs(config.input_dir)
    summary = make_base_summary(config, pdfs)

    output_path = Path(os.path.expanduser(config.output_xlsx)).resolve()
    if output_path.suffix.lower() != ".xlsx":
        summary["status"] = "failed"
        summary["limitations"].append("Requested output path does not end with .xlsx; wrapper will not write a mismatched format to a workbook path contract.")
        summary["next_steps"].append("Provide an output_xlsx path ending in .xlsx and rerun.")
        print(json.dumps(summary, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2))
        return 1

    delegate_script = resolve_delegate(config.preferred_base_script)
    summary["execution"]["delegate_script"] = str(delegate_script)

    if not delegate_script.exists():
        summary["status"] = "failed"
        summary["limitations"].append("Reviewed delegate script could not be resolved from the repository/script location.")
        summary["next_steps"].append("Ensure artifacts/scripts/pdf_to_excel_ocr.py exists at the reviewed repository path and rerun.")
        print(json.dumps(summary, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2))
        return 1

    if config.dry_run:
        summary["status"] = "partial"
        summary["limitations"].append("Dry-run requested; wrapper short-circuited before delegate execution to avoid overstating output production.")
        summary["next_steps"].append("Run again without --dry-run to attempt a real reviewed delegate conversion.")
        print(json.dumps(summary, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2))
        return 0

    if len(pdfs) == 0:
        summary["status"] = "partial"
        summary["limitations"].append("No PDF files were discovered under the provided input_dir using recursive discovery semantics.")
        summary["next_steps"].append("Add one or more PDF files under the input directory and rerun.")
        print(json.dumps(summary, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2))
        return 0

    output_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory(prefix="pdf_to_excel_runner_") as tmpdir:
        report_path = Path(tmpdir) / "delegate_report.json"
        cmd = build_delegate_command(config, delegate_script, report_path)
        summary["execution"]["delegated"] = True
        summary["execution"]["delegate_command"] = cmd

        try:
            completed = subprocess.run(
                cmd,
                stdout=subprocess.PIPE,
                stderr=subprocess.PIPE,
                text=True,
                timeout=config.timeout_seconds,
                check=False,
            )
        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired as exc:
            summary["status"] = "failed"
            summary["execution"]["delegate_exit_code"] = None
            summary["execution"]["delegate_timed_out"] = True
            summary["execution"]["stdout"] = exc.stdout
            summary["execution"]["stderr"] = exc.stderr
            summary["limitations"].append("Delegate execution timed out before completion.")
            summary["next_steps"].append("Rerun with a higher --timeout-seconds value only if the reviewed delegate is expected to complete successfully.")
            print(json.dumps(summary, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2))
            return 1

        summary["execution"]["delegate_exit_code"] = completed.returncode
        summary["execution"]["delegate_timed_out"] = False
        summary["execution"]["stdout"] = completed.stdout
        summary["execution"]["stderr"] = completed.stderr

        delegate_report = safe_json_loads(completed.stdout)
        if not isinstance(delegate_report, dict) and report_path.exists():
            try:
                delegate_report = json.loads(report_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
            except Exception:
                delegate_report = None

        if not isinstance(delegate_report, dict):
            summary["status"] = "failed"
            summary["limitations"].append("Reviewed delegate did not provide a parseable structured JSON report on stdout or via --report-json sidecar.")
            summary["next_steps"].append("Confirm the reviewed delegate supports --report-json and emits contract-compliant JSON fields such as status, total_files, status_counter, and dry_run.")
            print(json.dumps(summary, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2))
            return 1

        summary["execution"]["delegate_report"] = delegate_report
        wrapper_status, attestation, limitations, next_steps = evaluate_delegate_report(delegate_report, config.output_xlsx)
        summary["status"] = wrapper_status
        summary["attestation"] = attestation
        summary["limitations"].extend(limitations)
        summary["next_steps"].extend(next_steps)

        if wrapper_status == "success":
            print(json.dumps(summary, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2))
            return 0
        if wrapper_status == "partial":
            print(json.dumps(summary, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2))
            return 0
        print(json.dumps(summary, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2))
        return 1


if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())

review_contract.artifact_policy: Always include either `file_contents` for the review artifact path or `artifacts` referencing that exact path.
review_contract.fallback_policy: If evidence is insufficient, return `status=partial`, include explicit `errors`, still generate review artifact content, and set verdict=needs_revision.

constraints: Review must be grounded in produced automation artifact
When both wrapper and reviewed delegate snapshots are supplied, evaluate them as one end-to-end readonly pair instead of ignoring the delegate evidence.
Do not invent missing artifact content
Return a clear verdict: pass, fail, or needs_revision
Generate review artifact markdown for expected_outputs[0].path
Use artifact_snapshots when provided; avoid claiming access problems if snapshot content exists.
When both the generated wrapper and reviewed delegate snapshots are provided, review them together rather than silently narrowing scope to one file.
Return metadata.verdict with one of: pass, fail, needs_revision.
Always generate review artifact content for expected_outputs[0].path.
If evidence is insufficient, use partial + errors + verdict=needs_revision, but still output review artifact.

source.title: BL-20260324-014 live preview smoke sample 2026-03-24 (best-effort reviewable attempt)

acceptance_criteria: Produce a review artifact with explicit verdict (pass/fail/needs_revision)

result.summary: Reviewed the inbox runner and paired delegate together as an end-to-end readonly smoke path. The pair is generally well-structured and evidence-oriented, but the wrapper's delegate resolution logic is incorrect for its default path and would fail to locate the supplied delegate at runtime in the reviewed repository layout. Verdict: needs_revision.
notes: Review was grounded in supplied artifact snapshots for both scripts.
Evaluation covered wrapper and delegate together per review scope.
Review artifact includes explicit verdict and rationale.
